skill.name: ui-ux-designer
skill.description: Create interface designs, wireframes, and design systems. Masters user research, accessibility standards, and modern design tools. Specializes in design tokens, component libraries, and inclusive design. Use PROACTIVELY for design systems, user flows, or interface optimization.
skill.summary: Create interface designs, wireframes, and design systems. Masters user research, accessibility stand...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static findings as false positives. SKILL.md contains Markdown guidance for UI/UX design, and no executable code, cryptographic implementation, system reconnaissance, network access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear interface guidance that balances user needs, accessibility, and delivery constraints. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create structured UI/UX plans, wireframes, design systems, and research workflows.
actual_capabilities: Plans user flows, wireframes, and interface structures for web, mobile, and desktop products.
Creates design system guidance for tokens, components, documentation, governance, and handoff.
Applies accessibility standards, including contrast, keyboard navigation, screen reader needs, and inclusive language.
Builds user research plans, usability testing scripts, journey maps, and validation methods.
Improves information architecture, visual hierarchy, empty states, errors, loading states, and responsive behavior.
Documents design rationale and developer handoff details for implementation planning.
limitations: It provides design guidance and specifications, not direct editing inside Figma or other design tools.
It cannot replace real user research with representative participants.
Accessibility guidance still requires product-specific testing with assistive technologies.
Visual output depends on the context, assets, and implementation details supplied by the user.

use_cases.title: Create a Product Redesign Plan
use_cases.description: Turn product goals, user segments, and current pain points into flows, page structure, and validation steps.

use_cases.title: Build a Design System Foundation
use_cases.description: Define tokens, component rules, accessibility criteria, documentation needs, and governance for scalable UI work.

use_cases.title: Prepare Developer Handoff
use_cases.description: Convert interface decisions into implementation notes, responsive behavior, states, and acceptance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for quick interface feedback.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this screen for clarity, visual hierarchy, accessibility, and user flow issues. Give prioritized recommendations with rationale.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a User Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for a new product workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a user flow for [task] used by [audience]. Include entry points, key screens, decisions, errors, empty states, and success metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Component System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for reusable product UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a design system plan for [product]. Define tokens, component categories, accessibility rules, documentation structure, governance, and developer handoff requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Lead a Research-Driven Redesign
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for complex UX strategy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a research-driven redesign strategy for [product area]. Include assumptions, research methods, journey map, IA changes, prototype plan, usability test, and rollout metrics.

output_examples.input: Improve the onboarding flow for a B2B analytics dashboard.
output: A revised onboarding flow with setup steps, progressive disclosure, and role-based defaults.
Accessibility notes for forms, focus order, labels, and error recovery.
Success metrics covering activation, time to first insight, and completion rate.

output_examples.input: Create a design system plan for a multi-brand SaaS product.
output: A token structure for color, typography, spacing, elevation, radius, and motion.
Component documentation requirements for buttons, inputs, navigation, tables, and alerts.
Governance steps for review, versioning, contribution, adoption, and deprecation.

output_examples.input: Audit a mobile checkout experience for accessibility and conversion.
output: A prioritized issue list covering form clarity, tap targets, contrast, validation, and trust signals.
Recommended checkout states for loading, failure, empty cart, discount errors, and payment success.
A testing plan using usability sessions, analytics events, and A/B comparison.

best_practices: Provide product context, target users, platform constraints, and current problems before requesting design guidance.
Ask for accessibility, responsive behavior, edge cases, and empty states in every design task.
Validate recommendations with research, usability testing, analytics, or stakeholder review before implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a polished interface without sharing users, goals, constraints, and brand requirements.
Do not treat accessibility as a final checklist after visual design is complete.
Do not copy design trends without checking usability, content needs, performance, and product fit.

faq.question: Can this skill create production-ready visual files?
faq.answer: No. It creates design direction, specifications, plans, and review guidance that teams can implement in design or code tools.

faq.question: Does it support accessibility work?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for WCAG alignment, contrast, keyboard access, screen reader needs, cognitive accessibility, and inclusive patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with design systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can define tokens, components, documentation, governance, adoption workflows, and developer handoff requirements.

faq.question: Can it replace user research?
faq.answer: No. It can plan and analyze research, but teams still need real participants and product data.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What input improves results?
faq.answer: Share the audience, product goal, platform, screenshots or flows, accessibility targets, business constraints, and known user problems.
